findings.description: The entire typography hierarchy table (font assignments per level/channel) and the available-weights lists are not represented by any candidate rule. This is a large, distinct governance area (font-per-channel mandates, weight constraints) that has been dropped entirely rather than clustered.

payload.description: Font assignments per channel (Agenda/Nunito Sans/Aptos) and allowed weight sets per font, including the campaign-headline exception for live Agenda text in digital.

findings.description: This single rule id appears to conflate at least three distinct governance items: (1) the email CSS font-stack/fallback requirement (u_0010), (2) the concrete px/line-height type scale table for email (u_0011-u_0018, including the sub-rules on minimum 12px and footnote distinctiveness), and (3) a general (non-email-specific) hierarchy principle about not using two adjacent same-size/weight blocks (u_0020). These have different scope (email-only vs general) and different hardness/specificity and should be split into separate rules.

new_rules.description: Email font-stack fallback: Nunito Sans, Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported.
Email type scale table (H1-H3, body, secondary body, footnote) with px/line-height values and minimum 12px floor.
Never use two adjacent text blocks at same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

findings.description: Source units are explicitly tagged [GENERAL], signaling these facets apply beyond email-only context, but the candidate rule is scoped/named as email-specific ('email_type_scale_hierarchy') with no governance facet capturing the cross-channel [GENERAL] applicability tag.

The conditional escalation rule (line-height must increase to 160% for light text on dark backgrounds) is a distinct conditional/contextual constraint merged silently into the flat 'minimum 150%' rule, losing its own condition scope.

new_rule.description: Increase body line-height to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px) for light text on dark backgrounds.

findings.description: The explicit hard floor 'Never set running text below 12px' is a standalone must_not constraint not clearly represented as its own rule with correct polarity (prohibition) distinct from the type-scale table.

payload.description: Never set running text below 12px (ISI sizing excluded, owned by locked component).
